# Feature/Enhancement Pull Request

## Feature

Adds \_\_\_ feature (link to feature request issue or describe feature)

## Description

A clear description of the new feature or enhancement, including:

- What does it do?
- Why is it needed?
- How does it work?

## Affected Component(s)

- [ ] Frontend (Angular Control Panel)
- [ ] Overlays (new or enhanced)
- [ ] Backend (ASP.NET Core)
- [ ] Database/Migrations
- [ ] Documentation

## Feature Details

### User-Facing Changes

How will users interact with this feature?

### Technical Implementation

High-level overview of the technical approach:

### New Dependencies

Any new npm packages or NuGet packages added?

- Package 1: reason
- Package 2: reason

## Related

- Feature request issue: #\_\_\_
- Related PRs: \_\_\_
- Related features: \_\_\_

## Testing

### Test Scenarios

1. Scenario 1: ...
2. Scenario 2: ...
3. Edge case: ...

### How to Test

1. Test step 1
2. Test step 2
3. Test step 3

### Expected Behavior

What should happen when testing the feature?

## Files Changed

### New Files

- `path/to/new/feature.ts`
- `path/to/new/component.component.ts`

### Modified Files

- `path/to/existing/file.ts`
- `path/to/existing/file.cs`

## Screenshots or Demo

Add screenshots showing the new feature in action:

## Backward Compatibility

- [ ] Feature is fully backward compatible
- [ ] Breaking changes: \_\_\_ (describe if any)

## Documentation

- [ ] README updated
- [ ] Inline code comments added
- [ ] API documentation updated (if applicable)
- [ ] Deployment notes added (if needed)

## Checklist

- [ ] Feature is complete and tested
- [ ] Code follows repository conventions
- [ ] Documentation is up to date
- [ ] No breaking changes (or documented)
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] No new console errors or warnings
